architecture_patterns.mapping: Map config, components, pipeline topology, lifecycle gates, backpressure gates, and delivery gates.
architecture_patterns.name: Component pipeline
architecture_patterns.redaction: Expose component names and contracts; redact customer endpoints, secrets, and payload content.
architecture_patterns.works_for: Telemetry agents, ETL jobs, event routers, streaming processors, and plugin frameworks

architecture_patterns.mapping: Use public component contracts, fake config, health states, and delivery-policy examples.
architecture_patterns.name: Enterprise API-only review
architecture_patterns.redaction: Do not publish production telemetry, endpoint URLs, tokens, tenant names, or incident traces.
architecture_patterns.works_for: Platforms where reviewers cannot access databases or deployed services

artifacts.name: Receiver Tree
artifacts.purpose: Defines receiver interfaces, helpers, and built-in receiver components.

artifacts.name: Processor Tree
artifacts.purpose: Defines processors that transform, batch, limit, or otherwise mediate telemetry.

artifacts.name: Exporter Tree
artifacts.purpose: Defines exporters and exporter helper behavior.

artifacts.name: Connector Tree
artifacts.purpose: Defines connectors that can route telemetry between pipelines.

artifacts.name: Extension Tree
artifacts.purpose: Defines service extensions such as auth, zpages, memory limiters, and capabilities.

artifacts.name: Service Tree
artifacts.purpose: Coordinates configuration, pipelines, component lifecycle, telemetry, and host capabilities.

artifacts.name: Configuration Map Tree
artifacts.purpose: Loads and resolves configuration maps and providers.

artifacts.name: Docs Tree
artifacts.purpose: Documents collector behavior, proposals, and images.

bigger_picture: This example shows how to map a component pipeline repo. The system is a configurable telemetry service: configuration declares pipelines, receivers ingest signals, processors transform or batch them, connectors can bridge pipelines, exporters send telemetry onward, extensions add service capabilities, and service orchestration manages startup, shutdown, and observability.

edges.why: The service orchestrator starts pipelines from resolved configuration.
Processor output is delivered to exporters in configured pipelines.

gates.name: Configuration Validation Gate
gates.risk_boundary: The collector should not start an invalid pipeline.

rules.if: all referenced components exist and pipeline shape is valid
missing component, invalid signal type, or bad setting

gates.name: Component Lifecycle Gate
gates.risk_boundary: Telemetry should flow only after required components start successfully.

rules.if: component fails startup or dependency check

gates.name: Backpressure And Memory Gate
gates.risk_boundary: A collector should protect host resources under load.

gates.name: Export Delivery Gate
gates.risk_boundary: Telemetry delivery failures should follow retry and queue policy.

rules.if: temporary failure and retry policy allows

known_boundaries: This is a public educational map, not an official OpenTelemetry maintainer audit.
It maps the collector architecture at a high level, not every component or distribution.
Real enterprise reviews should use fake or redacted telemetry payloads.
A full audit should inspect exact config, component versions, tests, runtime metrics, and deployment policy.
one_line: The OpenTelemetry Collector routes telemetry through configurable receivers, processors, connectors, extensions, and exporters.

rebuild_recipe.goal: Check the OpenTelemetry Collector public repo manifest.
Generate the OpenTelemetry Collector system review report.

review_questions: How does configuration become a running telemetry pipeline?
Which gates prevent invalid config or failed components from processing telemetry?
Where are backpressure, memory, queue, retry, and delivery policies enforced?
How can a reviewer audit topology without seeing customer telemetry payloads?
Which component health and service telemetry artifacts would prove runtime behavior?

schemas.name: CollectorConfig
schemas.purpose: Declares which components exist and how telemetry flows through pipelines.

schemas.name: ReceiverContract
schemas.purpose: Describes an ingest component that accepts telemetry.

schemas.name: ProcessorContract
schemas.purpose: Describes transformation, batching, filtering, memory, or enrichment behavior.

schemas.name: ExporterContract
schemas.purpose: Describes where telemetry is sent and how failures are handled.

schemas.name: TelemetryBatch
schemas.purpose: Represents telemetry moving through a pipeline.

schemas.name: ComponentStatus
schemas.purpose: Represents component lifecycle and health state.

scope: A public-safe system map of the OpenTelemetry Collector open-source repository based on public source directories and documentation.

source_links.label: GitHub repository
source_links.notes: Primary public source used for repo identity and source paths.

source_links.label: OpenTelemetry Collector documentation
source_links.notes: Public docs for collector concepts and configuration.

source_links.label: OpenTelemetry project
source_links.notes: Public project context for telemetry standards.

systems.ideal_target: Every pipeline is explicit, valid, and inspectable before startup.
systems.name: Configuration System
systems.purpose: Loads and resolves collector configuration into service pipelines.
systems.truth_boundary: Configuration describes desired telemetry flow; startup gates decide whether it can run.

systems.ideal_target: Telemetry ingress is explicit and observable.
systems.name: Receiver Layer
systems.purpose: Accepts telemetry from external sources and injects it into pipelines.
systems.truth_boundary: A receiver only starts when configuration and lifecycle checks pass.

systems.ideal_target: Transformations are predictable, measurable, and resource-aware.
systems.name: Processor Layer
systems.purpose: Transforms, batches, limits, or filters telemetry before export.
systems.truth_boundary: Processor behavior is bounded by pipeline order and configuration.

systems.ideal_target: Optional capabilities are explicit and health-reporting.
systems.lifecycle: component config -> started connector/extension -> runtime capability
systems.name: Connector And Extension Layer
systems.purpose: Bridges pipelines and adds service-level capabilities.
systems.truth_boundary: Connectors and extensions can change topology or service behavior; they must start cleanly.

systems.ideal_target: Export behavior is reliable, backpressure-aware, and observable.
systems.name: Exporter Layer
systems.purpose: Sends processed telemetry to configured destinations.
systems.truth_boundary: Delivery depends on destination, queue, retry, and failure policy.

systems.ideal_target: A running collector is explainable from config to component status.
systems.lifecycle: valid config -> start components -> run pipelines -> shutdown
systems.name: Service Orchestrator
systems.purpose: Coordinates component lifecycle, pipelines, host capabilities, and service telemetry.
systems.truth_boundary: The service can expose health and telemetry, but configured components determine data path.

title: OpenTelemetry Collector Public Repo System Review Graph

walkthroughs.name: One telemetry batch through a pipeline
walkthroughs.story: A config declares a pipeline. The service starts components. A receiver accepts telemetry, processors transform or batch it, connectors can route it, and exporters deliver it with retry and queue policies.

walkthroughs.name: How to audit without customer telemetry
walkthroughs.story: A reviewer can use fake config and public component contracts to understand topology, gates, and failure handling without seeing production payloads.

workflows.name: Start Components
workflows.purpose: Start receivers, processors, exporters, connectors, and extensions.

workflows.name: Receive Telemetry
workflows.purpose: Bring traces, metrics, or logs into a configured pipeline.

workflows.name: Process Telemetry
workflows.purpose: Apply configured processing and protect host resources.

workflows.name: Route Or Export
workflows.purpose: Send telemetry onward or route it through another pipeline.

workflows.name: Observe Collector
workflows.purpose: Expose service status and runtime telemetry for operators.
